end of world model.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board changed (board_changed: true) but the level wasn't completed. Let me check what actually changed by comparing the before and after frames. The purple bar might have changed, or something else might have happened.
Let me compare the segmentation between the previous and current frames to see what changed.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: The board changed but the level wasn't completed. Let me check what actually changed when I clicked the purple bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after frames to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

# Find differences
print("BEFORE nodes:")
for nid, n in sorted(before_nodes.items()):
    print(f"  id={nid} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']}")

print("\nAFTER nodes:")
for nid, n in sorted(after_nodes.items()):
    print(f"  id={nid} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']}")

# Check for changes in node properties
print("\nChanges:")
for nid in before_nodes:
    if nid in after_nodes:
        bn = before_nodes[nid]
        an = after_nodes[nid]
        if bn['hash'] != an['hash'] or bn['color'] != an['color'] or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels']:
            print(f"  Node {nid}: color {bn['color']}->{an['color']}, pixels {bn['pixels']}->{an['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
